briefdescription.para: Maximum tap value.
Driver's minor version number.
Driver's major version number.
API initialises one instance of an Equalizer driver.

detaileddescription.para: Traverse "/sys/bus/platform/device" directory (in Linux), to find registered EQU device with the name DeviceNodeName. The first available slot in the instances array XDfeEqu_Equalizer[] will be taken as a DeviceNodeName object. On success it moves the state machine to a Ready state, while on failure stays in a Not Ready state.

listitem.para: Pointer to the instance if successful.

briefdescription.para: API closes the instance of an Equalizer driver and moves the state machine to a Not Ready state.
Resets Equalizer and puts block into a reset state.
Reads configuration from device tree/xparameters.h and IP registers.

detaileddescription.para: Removes S/W reset and moves the state machine to a Configured state.

briefdescription.para: DFE Equalizer driver one time initialisation and moves the state machine to a Initialised state.
Activates channel Equalizer moves the state machine to an Activated state.
Deactivates Equalizer and moves the state machine to Initialised state.
Gets a state machine state id.
Updates Equalizer mode.

detaileddescription.para: Config.DatapathMode defines real or complex Equalizer mode. Config.RealDatapathSet and Config.ImDatapathSet defines which coefficient set to be used in real or complex mode. Config.Flush = 1 instructs to flush the buffers.

briefdescription.para: Returns current trigger configuration.
Sets trigger configuration.

memberdef.argsstring: (const XDfeEqu *InstancePtr, u32 ChannelField, u32 Mode, u32 Shift, const XDfeEqu_Coefficients *EqCoeffs)
memberdef.name: XDfeEqu_LoadCoefficients

briefdescription.para: Sets Equalizer filter coefficients in Real, Complex or Matrix mode.

parameterdescription.para: Flag in which bits indicate the channel is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Gets used coefficients settings.
Sets the delay, which will be added to TUSER and TLAST (delay matched through the IP).
Reads the delay, which will be added to TUSER and TLAST (delay matched through the IP).
Returns data latency + tap, where the tap is between 0 and 23 in real mode and between 0 and 11 in complex mode.
This API gets the driver and HW design version.
Contains the APIs for the DFE Equalizer Filter component.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 dc 09/03/20 Initial version dc 02/02/21 Remove hard coded device node name dc 02/22/21 align driver to current specification dc 03/15/21 Add data latency api dc 04/06/21 Register with full node name dc 04/07/21 Fix bare metal initialisation dc 04/20/21 Doxygen documentation update dc 05/08/21 Update to common trigger 1.1 dc 05/26/21 Update CFG_SHIFT calculation dc 07/13/21 Update to common latency requirements dc 10/26/21 Make driver R5 compatible 1.2 dc 10/29/21 Update doxygen comments dc 11/09/21 Add GetStateId API dc 11/19/21 Update doxygen documentation dc 12/17/21 Update after documentation review 1.3 dc 01/19/22 Assert Update trigger dc 01/24/22 Auto-compute number of units dc 02/18/22 Write 1 clears event status dc 03/21/22 Add prefix to global variables 1.4 dc 04/06/22 Update documentation 1.5 cog 07/04/23 Add support for SDT 1.6 cog 01/29/24 Yocto SDT support 1.7 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors dc 04/10/26 Correct Complex mode configuration
